name: River runout JhTh9c-8h-7c wet
description: Wet runout: flop JhTh9c, turn 8h, river 7c. Board shows a 5-card straight (7-8-9-T-J). Many hands make straights (any 8 or higher), flushes (any hearts), or nearly-nut hands. Tests solver behavior when the board itself has a strong hand and hero/villain play for kickers and blockers. Ranges use rank-based notation only (the range parser doesn't accept specific-card combos like AhJh). Hero = BB postflop caller, villain = BTN SRP.

input.hero_range: JJ, TT, 99, 88, AJs, ATs, KJs, KTs, QJs, QTs, JTs, AQs, AKs, KQs, AJo, ATo, KJo, KTo, QJo, QTo, JTo
input.villain_range: QQ, KK, AA, AQs, AKs, KQs, AJs, ATs, KJs, KTs, QJs, QTs, JTs, 88, AJo, AQo, AKo, KQo, KJo

expected_notes: Populated by differential run. The board is a 5-card straight 7-8-9-T-J, so any A/K/Q makes a higher straight; flush with two hearts; made trips / two-pair with 88 and JJ/TT/99. Expect high value-bet frequency from villain's QQ/AA (which have been navigating to showdown) and heavy polarization.
